extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked on final climb, dropped FROOME by 1:30+ and DUMOULIN, extending GC lead decisively
tactical_patterns: Yates attacked when Dumoulin finished pulling on final climb, isolating him after he'd emptied himself working
Karapaz/Poels responded for Movistar when Dumoulin faltered, revealing team split priorities between stage/GC
Froome's minute-plus loss suggests Zoncolan stage hangover manifests next-day, not same-day weakness pattern
Large breakaway group accepted early suggests peloton conceded stage to preserve energy for mountain finish

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: extended GC lead by attacking precisely when domestique Dumoulin finished working the climb
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: lost 1:30+ suggesting previous stage mega-effort creates next-day rather than immediate weakness
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: did pulling work then couldn't follow attacks, burned bonuses chasing instead of securing podium
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: responded strongly to attacks after appearing questionable earlier in stage
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: neutral: contested intermediate sprint in breakaway group showing sprint form returning
